title: vandenboschia boschiana
intro: Vandenboschia boschiana, also known as the rock cap fern or Bosch's vandenboschia, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help to soothe wounds and prevent infection. The health benefits of Vandenboschia boschiana include its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Vandenboschia boschiana are typically made by infusing the plant's leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be used topically or taken orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
